🔬 Science

Life Process

Discover how living things grow, breathe, eat, and reproduce!

📚 Unit 5 ⏱️ 9 min read

Unit 5: Life Process

Life processes are the activities that all living things do to stay alive. These include nutrition, respiration, transportation, excretion, growth, and reproduction.

The Seven Life Processes (MRS GREN)

LetterProcessWhat it means
MMovementChanging position
RRespirationUsing oxygen to release energy
SSensitivityResponding to changes
GGrowthBecoming larger
RReproductionProducing young ones
EExcretionRemoving waste
NNutritionTaking in food

1. Nutrition

Nutrition is how organisms get and use food.

Types of Nutrition

TypeHow they get foodExamples
Autotrophic (Self-feeding)Make their own food using sunlightGreen plants
Heterotrophic (Other-feeding)Eat other organismsAnimals, humans, fungi

Photosynthesis (How Plants Make Food)

Photosynthesis = Photo (light) + Synthesis (to make)

Equation:

Carbon dioxide + Water + Sunlight → Glucose + Oxygen

Requirements for Photosynthesis:

RequirementWhere it comes from
SunlightSun
Carbon dioxideAir (through leaves)
WaterSoil (through roots)
ChlorophyllGreen pigment in leaves

Products of Photosynthesis:

ProductWhat happens to it
Glucose (food)Used for energy or stored
OxygenReleased into the air

💡 Did you know? Plants are called producers because they produce their own food.

Human Nutrition (Balanced Diet)

A balanced diet contains all the nutrients your body needs.

NutrientFunctionSources
CarbohydratesGive energyRice, bread, potato
ProteinsBuild and repair bodyEggs, meat, beans, milk
FatsStore energy, keep warmOil, butter, nuts
VitaminsProtect from diseasesFruits, vegetables
MineralsStrong bones and teethMilk, salt, greens
WaterCarry nutrients, remove wasteWater, juices
FiberHelp digestionVegetables, whole grains

The Food Pyramid

🍭 FATS & OILS
Eat Least
🥛 Milk • 🥚 Eggs
🍖 Meat • 🫘 Beans
Eat Moderately
🥦 Vegetables • 🍎 Fruits
Eat More
🍚 Rice • 🍞 Bread • 🌾 Cereals
Eat Most

2. Respiration

Respiration is the process of releasing energy from food.

Respiration vs. Breathing

BreathingRespiration
Physical processChemical process
Inhale and exhale airEnergy is released
Happens in lungsHappens in cells

Types of Respiration

TypeOxygen neededProductsEnergy
AerobicYesCO₂ + Water + EnergyLots
AnaerobicNoLactic acid + EnergyLittle

Equation for Aerobic Respiration

Glucose + Oxygen → Carbon dioxide + Water + Energy

The Human Respiratory System

PartFunction
NoseFilters and warms air
Windpipe (Trachea)Carries air to lungs
LungsExchange gases (O₂ in, CO₂ out)
DiaphragmHelps pull air in and out

3. Transportation

Transportation is moving substances around the body.

Human Circulatory System

PartFunction
HeartPumps blood
BloodCarries oxygen, nutrients, waste
Blood vessels (arteries, veins, capillaries)Carry blood throughout the body

Path of Blood:

Heart → Arteries → Body parts → Veins → Heart

Transportation in Plants

Plants transport water and food through special tubes.

TubeWhat it carriesDirection
XylemWater and mineralsRoots → Upwards
PhloemFood (sugar)Leaves → All parts

4. Excretion

Excretion is removing waste products from the body.

Human Excretory System

Waste ProductHow it is removedOrgan
Carbon dioxideBreathing outLungs
SweatSweatingSkin
UrineUrinationKidneys, bladder

Excretion in Plants

Waste ProductHow it is removed
OxygenReleased through leaves (stomata)
Excess waterTranspiration (evaporation from leaves)
Old leavesFall off

5. Growth

Growth is the increase in size of an organism.

Growth in Plants

StageDescription
SeedDormant (sleeping)
GerminationSeed sprouts
SeedlingSmall plant appears
AdultFully grown plant
FloweringProduces flowers
Fruit and seedsProduces new seeds

What plants need to grow:

NeedPurpose
SunlightPhotosynthesis
WaterTransport and photosynthesis
Air (CO₂)Photosynthesis
NutrientsFrom soil
SpaceTo spread roots and leaves

Growth in Humans

StageAge (approx.)Changes
Infancy0-2 yearsRapid growth
Childhood3-11 yearsSteady growth
Adolescence12-18 yearsPuberty, growth spurt
Adulthood19+ yearsGrowth stops

6. Reproduction

Reproduction is producing offspring (young ones).

Types of Reproduction

TypeHow it worksExamples
AsexualOne parent, identical offspringBacteria, some plants
SexualTwo parents, different offspringHumans, animals, most plants

Reproduction in Plants

TypeDescriptionExamples
Seed reproductionSeeds grow into new plantsMango, beans
Spore reproductionSpores grow into new plantsFerns, moss
Vegetative propagationNew plants from stems, roots, leavesPotato (stem), onion (bulb)

Parts of a Flower:

PartFunction
PetalAttracts insects
SepalProtects the flower bud
Stamen (male)Produces pollen
Pistil (female)Receives pollen, contains ovules
OvaryBecomes fruit
OvuleBecomes seed

Pollination: Transfer of pollen from stamen to pistil.

Reproduction in Animals

TypeDescriptionExamples
OviparousLay eggsBirds, reptiles, fish, frogs
ViviparousGive birth to live youngHumans, dogs, cats, cows

7. Sensitivity (Response to Stimuli)

Sensitivity is the ability to detect and respond to changes in the environment.

Stimulus = a change in the environment. Response = the reaction to the stimulus.

OrganismStimulusResponse
PlantSunlightGrows towards the sun (phototropism)
PlantTouch (tendril)Wraps around support
HumanHeatPulls hand away
HumanLoud soundCovers ears
EarthwormLightMoves away from light

💡 Phototropism: Plants growing toward light.

Try It Yourself!

Exercise 1: Fill in the Blanks

  1. The process by which plants make their own food is called ____________.
  2. The equation for photosynthesis is: CO₂ + H₂O + ____________ → Glucose + ____________.
  3. The gas we breathe in is ____________. The gas we breathe out is ____________.
  4. The ____________ pumps blood throughout the body.
  5. Animals that lay eggs are called ____________.

Exercise 2: Match the Nutrient to Its Function

NutrientFunction
Carbohydratesa) Builds muscles
Proteinsb) Gives energy
Vitaminsc) Protects from diseases
Fatsd) Stores energy

Exercise 3: True or False

  1. Plants do not need oxygen. (T/F)
  2. The human heart has four chambers. (T/F)
  3. Viviparous animals lay eggs. (T/F)
  4. Xylem transports food in plants. (T/F)
  5. Respiration releases energy from food. (T/F)

Exercise 4: Label the Parts of a Flower

Write the name of each part.

  1. Male part of flower → ____________
  2. Female part of flower → ____________
  3. Becomes fruit → ____________
  4. Becomes seed → ____________
  5. Attracts insects → ____________

Answer Key

Exercise 1: 1. photosynthesis, 2. Sunlight, Oxygen; 3. oxygen, carbon dioxide; 4. heart; 5. oviparous

Exercise 2: Carbohydrates→b, Proteins→a, Vitamins→c, Fats→d

Exercise 3: 1. F, 2. T, 3. F, 4. F, 5. T

Exercise 4: 1. Stamen, 2. Pistil, 3. Ovary, 4. Ovule, 5. Petal

Great work! You now understand the amazing processes that keep living things alive!
